linewidth.help: width of the vertical line if it is drawn

linecolor.help: color of the vertical line if it is drawn

font.help: the characteristics of the text to be drawn please see directive <plotfont>

display.help: <display> is a list of items the user decides to display. If it is empty, nothing is shown. Add as many keywords as required.

date_format.help: If the date is displayed, this is the format for drawing it. You can get help on date formats by typing 'man strftime'

position.help: This determines where the text will go and how it will be organised. For 'top' and 'bottom' all the texts are drawn horizontally, they may overlap. For 'top_indented' and 'bottom_indented', the text is drawn horizontally, but if there is an overlap, the text is 'crenelled' or indented. For 'top_angled' and 'bottom_angled', the text is drawn at an angle (please see the keyword <angle>), this is the safest and most readable way of drawing text without overlaps. The text may cover the contents of the plot though, if this is the case, a good move is to set a negative <rank> to the comment, so that it is drawn before the contents of the plot. For 'best_angled' and 'best_indented', the application decideds whether the text should go at the top or at the bottom of the plot, depending on available space

angle.help: Determines the angle used to display angled text. The bigger the angle, the longer the text can be, but the more risks to cover the contents of the plot
